title: epiphyllum phyllanthus subsp. rubrocoronatum
intro: Epiphyllum phyllanthus subsp. rubrocoronatum is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant, belonging to the cactus family (Cactaceae) and is often referred to as an epiphytic cactus. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Epiphyllum phyllanthus subsp. rubrocoronatum are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots or stems, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
